channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Edo APC hails LG poll, pledges 2.5 million votes for Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Edo APC celebrates its overwhelming victory in the local government elections, affirming its strong position for 2027 and pledging 2.5 million votes for Pr
Read More: https://punchng.com/edo-apc-hails-lg-poll-pledges-2-5-million-votes-for-tinubu/

item.title: Xenophobia: Okpebholo pledges N41m support for Edo returnees from South Africa
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Edo State Governor, Monday Okpebholo, pledges N41 million to support 41 Edo indigenes returning from South Africa after xenophobic attacks, offering N1m ea
Read More: https://punchng.com/xenophobia-okpebholo-pledges-n41m-support-for-edo-returnees-from-south-africa/

item.dc:creator: Leadership

The affected trad

item.title: UK designates Iran’s Revolutionary Guard national security threat
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The United Kingdom government has designated Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) a national security threat, introducing new powers that make supporting the organisation a criminal offence punishable by up to 14 years in prison. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood announced the measures

item.title: Russia’s Oil Output Falls to Lowest in at Least 2 1/2 Years
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Russia’s crude output declined to the lowest in at least two and a half years in June as Ukraine attacked the nation’s oil infrastructure on an almost daily basis.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Europe’s sovereign-debt dynamics are in danger of going badly awry if the region doesn’t get a grip on its public finances, the International Monetary Fund said.

item.title: One person killed in Maine shooting involving ICE, state lawmaker says
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: One person was killed in Maine in a fatal shooting involving US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), the speaker of the Maine House of Representatives said Monday. The incident came days after an ICE officer shot and killed a man at a traffic stop in Houston, Texas.

item.title: Appeal Court Bars INEC From Recognising Mark-Led ADC Congresses
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: It held that congresses and the national convention conducted by the Mark-led ADC amounted to a nullity, as they were held in disobedience to a subsisting order that the high court made on April 14. The post Appeal Court Bars INEC From Recognising Mark-Led ADC Congresses appeared first on Channels T

item.title: WAFCON 2026: Oshoala, Rasheedat, Seven Others Arrive As Super Falcons Camp Opens in Casablanca
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria’s preparations for the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) got underway on Monday as nine players, including captain Asisat Oshoala and experienced forward Rasheedat Ajibade, arrived in Casablanca, Morocco, for the opening of the Super Falcons’ training camp. The te

item.title: Blogger allegedly linked to false reports against Soludo, family remanded
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Police say defendant was arrested in Imo State By Vincent Ujumadu AWKA — A blogger and publisher of Igbo Times Magazine and INews, Ejike Ofoegbu, has been remanded in a correctional centre in Anambra State after he was arraigned before a Magistrate Court in Awka over alleged cyberstalking, defamati

item.title: FCT Police Arraign Three Over Alleged Murder in Abuja
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Police Command has arraigned three suspects before the FCT High Court over the alleged murder of Adimike Godwin in the Guzape area of Abuja. The defendants — Adimike Odirachukwu Anthony, Adimike Chinyere Stephany and Comfort Ajibade — were arraigned before FCT Hig

item.title: Arm And Marvell Tumble As Chip Stocks Sell Off
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: ‘We need more players’ — new Liverpool boss Iraola
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "Selfishly, I want the players here from day one for pre-season, but football doesn't work like that. The club is working hard to get those signings done." The post ‘We need more players’ — new Liverpool boss Iraola appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Facing Digital Threats, MGM Resorts Trades Higher On Reported Takeover Talks With Barry Diller's People Inc.
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Trump slams 20% security charges on cargo shipped through Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: United States President, Donald Trump on Monday, announced that his administration would impose a 20 per cent security charge on all cargo shipped through the Strait of Hormuz, saying the levy would cover the cost of ensuring safety and security in the strategic waterway. In a post on Truth Social,

item.title: India thrash England in historic first women’s cricket Test match at Lord’s
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: England, set a record-breaking target of 457 for victory, were dismissed for 186 shortly before lunch on the final day.

item.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Posts Stronger-Than-Expected June Sales
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: More Americans are struggling to repay credit card bills for groceries as costs rise
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: How a Nigerian engineer expands innovation across frontiers of space exploration
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Tech
item.description: Robert Okojie, a Nigerian-born engineer, has built a career at the forefront of space technology, earning global recognition for inventions.. read more How a Nigerian engineer expands innovation across frontiers of space exploration

item.title: Gowon: Buhari Governed Nigeria Without Regional Bias, Treated All Equally
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: ‎Former Head of State, General Yakubu Gowon, has said late former President Muhammadu Buhari governed Nigeria without regional bias, insisting that he treated every state and region fairly rather than favouring the North as widely perceived. ‎ ‎Speaking at the first-year remembrance of Buhari held i

item.title: Dutch referee dies weeks after being dropped from World Cup
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Dutch football referee Rob Dieperink dies aged 38, weeks after he was dropped from officiating at the World Cup because of a police investigation in the UK.

item.title: UBS Sees Goldman, JPMorgan in Focus on Busy Earnings Day
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: UBS Group AG analyst Erika Najarian says Goldman Sachs Group Inc. may have the tallest order when it comes to demonstrating earnings prowess this week, while investors may be focused on more on succession at JPMorgan Chase & Co. She previews Wall Street bank earnings on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (So
